audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in references.c are guarded: `ldap_count_references` uses a loop condition `chain != NULL` before any deref; `ldap_first_reference` and `ldap_next_reference` rely on `assert(chain/ref != NULL)` — callers in back-meta, back-asyncmeta, back-ldap, and ldapsearch always pass non-NULL messages from `ldap_result()`. The critical `AC_MEMCPY(&be, ref->lm_ber, sizeof(be))` in `ldap_parse_reference` is safe because `lm_ber` is only set in `result.c:997` after an explicit null guard at `result.c:924-926` (`if (ber == NULL) return(rc)`), making it impossible for a LDAP_RES_SEARCH_REFERENCE message to carry a NULL `lm_ber`.
